title: Confession Study Guide
wordBank: Reconciliation and Anointing of the Sick

sentences.text: "Through the ministry of the Church may God give you pardon and peace, I absolve you of your sins in the name of the Father, Son, H.S" is all part of the [Form] of the sacrament.
Contrition for sins, confession of sin to ordained priest, acts of penance are the [Matter] of the sacrament
The minister of Confession is an [Ordained Bishop or Priest]
First condition for sin to be mortal: is a [Grave Matter]
Second condition for sin to be mortal: [Full Knowledge]
Third condition for sin to be mortal: [Deliberate Consent]
First way other than confession a venial sin can be forgiven: [Pious Act]
Second way a venial sin can be forgiven other than Confession: [Prayer]
Good way to tell if sin is a grave matter: Did I do this out of [malice]?
Jesus gave his apostles the power to [forgive sins] after his resurrection.
Perfect contrition is sorrow for sin based on [love for God]
Imperfect contrition is based on [fear of punishment]
Effect of Confession: [Reconciliation] with God
Effect of Confession: Reconciliation with the [Church]
Effect of Confession: Recovery of the state of [grace]
Effect of Confession: Mortal sin does not give eternal [punishment]
Effect of Confession: Part of the [temporal] punishment is relieved
Effect of Confession: [Peace and serenity of conscience]
Effect of Confession: Increase in spiritual [strength] for the struggle of Christian living
The two sacraments of healing: [Reconciliation and Anointing of the Sick].
sentences.answer: Reconciliation and Anointing of the Sick
